pattern: An ordered multiset supports search, insert, and delete-one while tolerating duplicates, which is exactly the contract a skiplist provides.
hints: Search, add, and erase all reduce to operations on a sorted collection that may contain repeats.
Erase must remove only one copy and report whether anything was removed, so distinguish 'not present' from 'present and removed'.
Keep the values in sorted order and locate the insertion or removal point by binary search, shifting as needed; duplicates simply sit next to each other.

edge_inputs.description: add, search hits and misses, then erase
search on an empty skiplist returns false
duplicates: erase removes only one copy
erase of an absent value returns false and changes nothing
boundary values at both ends of the range

stress.description: randomized add, search, and erase over a small value space so duplicates and hits arise
stress.note: small value range makes erase and search frequently hit existing values
